subject: Geology
title: Wilson Cycle
description: A quick win: understand Wilson Cycle.

hook.text: Oceans are born, grow, shrink, and die. The Atlantic is young and growing. The Pacific is old and shrinking.

buildup.text: The Wilson Cycle describes the life of an ocean basin from rifting to spreading to subduction to final closure.

discovery.text: Named after J. Tuzo Wilson, the cycle shows that every ocean eventually closes as plates shift direction.

twist.text: The Mediterranean Sea is a remnant of the ancient Tethys Ocean, now in its closing stage as Africa pushes into Europe.

climax.text: The Wilson Cycle connects rifting, seafloor spreading, subduction, and collision into one elegant sequence.

quiz.question: What does the Wilson Cycle describe?
options: The life cycle of an ocean basin from birth to closure
